description: Agent 12 — Collections & Recovery Agent payment plan and settlement configuration reference

fdcpa_time_restrictions.enforcement: Python datetime comparison with pytz timezone conversion — not LLM

7_in_7_rule.description: No more than 7 telephone calls within 7 consecutive days per debt

post_conversation_wait.description: No telephone call within 7 days after a telephone conversation with the consumer

electronic_communication.limited_content_messages: Voicemail may use limited content message that does not reveal debt collection purpose

required_content: Name of creditor to whom debt is owed
Consumer right to dispute within 30 days
Verification will be obtained if disputed
Name and address of original creditor upon written request

scra_provisions.applicability: Pre-service debt only; rate cap applies retroactively to date of active duty
scra_provisions.required_action: Reduce interest rate to 6% max; recalculate balance; credit excess interest paid
scra_provisions.documentation: Written notice and copy of military orders required from servicemember

bankruptcy_provisions.effect: All collection activity must immediately stop upon bankruptcy filing
exceptions: Secured creditor relief after § 362(d) motion approved by court
permitted_actions: File proof of claim with bankruptcy court

settlement_tiers.description: Settlement discount tiers by balance and authorization level
settlement_tiers.python_constants: SETTLEMENT_TIERS in agent/state.py — not configurable at runtime

TIER_1.notes: Standard collector authority for fresh accounts

TIER_2.notes: Most common settlement tier — 35% off is standard industry offer

TIER_3.notes: High discount for aged or difficult accounts

TIER_4.notes: Maximum settlement — SOL-expired or litigation-adverse accounts

high_value_threshold.description: Settlement amount >$10K OR discount >40% triggers SETTLEMENT_HIGH_VALUE HITL condition

credit_reporting.regulation: Fair Credit Reporting Act — 15 U.S.C. § 1681 et seq.
credit_reporting.notes: CFPB proposed rule (2025) would remove medical debt under $500 from credit reports

always_hitl_conditions.description: 9 conditions that always require human review — Python frozenset, immutable at runtime

conditions.SCRA_DETECTED: Active military — SCRA interest rate cap, all collection requires supervisor
conditions.BANKRUPTCY_STAY_DETECTED: Automatic stay — ALL collection must stop, escalate to compliance
conditions.DISPUTE_RECEIVED: Consumer disputed debt — validation notice + 30-day collection hold
conditions.CEASE_DESIST_RECEIVED: C&D received — only legal action notice permitted per FDCPA § 805(c)
conditions.DECEASED_ACCOUNT: Consumer is deceased — estate/executor procedures, state probate law
conditions.SETTLEMENT_HIGH_VALUE: Settlement >$10K or discount >40% — supervisor authorization required
conditions.LITIGATION_HIGH_RISK: Legal review before any further collection action
conditions.REGULATORY_COMPLAINT: CFPB/state AG complaint received — compliance officer review
conditions.MINOR_ACCOUNT: Debtor under 18 — legal guardian required, state consumer protection law

state_sol_notes.description: Statute of limitations varies by state and debt category
important_notes: SOL does not eliminate the debt — it limits the right to sue
Threatening to sue on time-barred debt violates FDCPA § 807(2)(A) — prohibited false representation
Collecting on time-barred debt voluntarily paid by consumer is generally permissible
SOL clock restarts from date of last payment in most states
Some states (NY, WI) bar collection activity entirely on time-barred debt — check state law
